cover.headline: Spring Boot Developer Productivity
cover.subhead: Benchmarks for build time, test coverage, and deployment frequency across real teams.
cover.footnote: 100+ teams surveyed · anonymized · segmented by team size and industry

about.intro: How long does a Spring Boot build really take in 2026? How much of your code is actually covered by tests? How often does the average team ship to production? This report turns first-hand answers from 100+ engineering teams into honest, comparable benchmarks — so you can see where your team stands, and where the rest of the industry is heading.

buildTimes.headline: Build times scale faster than teams do
buildTimes.intro: We asked teams for the median wall-clock time of a clean `./mvnw verify` (or Gradle equivalent) on the developer machine they use day-to-day. Larger teams report meaningfully longer builds — driven mostly by test-suite weight, not compilation.

takeaway.title: The 3.6× rule
takeaway.body: On average, teams of 20+ developers wait 3.6× longer for a green build than 1–5 dev teams. Most of the gap is test execution: 71% of the larger teams report integration tests as the dominant slice of build time.

testCoverage.headline: Test coverage clusters around 'good enough'
testCoverage.intro: Line coverage, self-reported from each team's primary Spring Boot service. The distribution is sharply peaked: most teams have decided 60–80% is the right operating point and stopped optimising.

pullQuote.text: We stopped chasing coverage at 75%. The last 20% would have taken longer to write than the entire previous year of features.

deployFrequency.headline: Most teams ship weekly — very few ship on demand
deployFrequency.intro: Production deployment cadence for the team's primary service. 'On demand' means a developer can deploy any commit, any time, with no batch boundary.

closing.title: What's next
closing.body: The full 2026 report will include segmentation by industry (finance, e-commerce, public sector, SaaS), team age, and CI provider — plus the open-ended answers from teams that have measurably moved from monthly to weekly cadence inside the past 12 months.
closing.ctaTitle: Want to contribute next year's data?
closing.ctaBody: Take the 2027 survey (~4 minutes, fully anonymous) and you'll receive the next report the moment it ships.
